anti_patterns.name: God Aggregate / Monolithic State
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-core/src/editor_state.rs -- EditorState struct with 40+ public fields spanning buffer, cursor, viewport, modal state, macro state, search state, jump/change lists, rendering hooks, and plugin panels. Any new subsystem forces a shotgun surgery across this one struct.

anti_patterns.name: Anemic Domain Model
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-core/src/ -- Buffer, Cursor, Viewport, and Theme are data containers; all behavior lives in module-level free functions (e.g., buffer::insert, cursor::move_right) rather than methods on the types. Aligns with Rust idioms but results in domain behaviour scattered outside the aggregate.

anti_patterns.name: Domain Logic in Imperative Shell
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-tui/src/input.rs and app.rs -- InputState, Operator, DeferredAction enums and execute_motion logic represent core modal-editing domain concepts (operator-pending state, motion resolution, register selection) that live in the TUI shell instead of alfred-core. This prevents unit testing without a terminal.

anti_patterns.name: Missing Typed Domain Events
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-core/src/hook.rs -- the hook system uses stringly-typed names ('render-gutter', 'on-save', 'render-status') with no typed event enum or constant. Hook contracts are undiscoverable; a typo silently produces no dispatch.

anti_patterns.name: Shared Kernel Overload
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-core/src/ -- the core kernel packs 14 modules covering unrelated concerns: buffer, cursor, rainbow_csv, theme, text_object, viewport, panel, hook, command, key_event, error, editor_state, facade, handlers. rainbow_csv in particular is a feature plugin concern that leaked into the domain kernel.

context_map_mermaid:
graph TD
  Core["alfred-core\n(Pure Domain Kernel)\nBuffer, Cursor, Viewport,\nCommands, Keymaps, Hooks,\nTheme, Panels, EditorState"]
  Lisp["alfred-lisp\n(FFI Anti-Corruption Layer)\nLispRuntime, Bridge primitives"]
  Plugin["alfred-plugin\n(Plugin Lifecycle)\nDiscovery, Registry, Metadata"]
  Syntax["alfred-syntax\n(Supporting - Syntax Highlighting)\nSyntaxHighlighter, LanguageConfig"]
  TUI["alfred-tui\n(Imperative Shell)\nEvent loop, Renderer, InputState"]
  Bin["alfred-bin\n(Composition Root)\nmain, run_editor, load_plugins"]
  Plugins["plugins/\n(Generic Extension Layer)\nvim-keybindings, status-bar,\nline-numbers, browse-mode, etc."]

  Core -->|"depended on by"| Lisp
  Core -->|"depended on by"| Plugin
  Core -->|"depended on by"| Syntax
  Core -->|"depended on by"| TUI
  Lisp -->|"depended on by"| Plugin
  Lisp -->|"depended on by"| TUI
  Lisp -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  Plugin -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  TUI -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  Syntax -->|"depended on by"| TUI
  Syntax -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  Bin -->|"loads at runtime"| Plugins
  Plugins -->|"calls via bridge"| Lisp
